$description.$value: Core primitive tokens for Nango. Neutral ramp aligned to the actual Nango app charcoal grays (warm-neutral, not zinc). Brand and info both use the Nango cyan-blue ramp (#00B2E3 family — from webapp index.css). Success uses Nango green (#4CD964 family), warning uses Nango yellow (#F7C752 family), danger uses Nango red (#EE4242 family). Geist for UI text, Geist Mono for code/data. Primitives are shared across themes — semantic files resolve them per mode.
Shared primitive color palette sourced from the Nango webapp CSS (packages/webapp/src/index.css).
Absolute anchors.
Transparent for ghost/overlay states.

Warm-neutral charcoal ramp. Light end fixed for clear visual steps: 0=#FFF, 25=#FAFAFA, 50=#F5F5F5, 100=#EBEBEB, 200=#E0E0E0. Dark stops: 975=#000 (canvas), 950=#0B0B0C (page), 925=#121212 (panel), 800=#2A2B2F (overlay). Sourced from Nango webapp charcoal palette.

925-50.$description: neutral.925 at 50% opacity — for alert.bg in dark mode.

750.$description: AA-safe brand stop, added to fix the primary button. .700 (#0089B0) is the marketing/website blue but only reaches 4.03:1 with white text — fails WCAG AA (4.5:1). .800 (#016886) passes at 6.31:1 but reads as dark teal, drifting from the brand cyan. At .750, white text hits 4.78:1 (AA pass with margin) while staying visibly closer to brand.500 (#00B2E3) than .800 does. Used as the single interactive.primary fill in BOTH Light and Dark themes so the button no longer changes hue between modes.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Nango brand cyan — the single source of truth for blue in the system. Brand 500 (#00B2E3) is the canonical Nango cyan, used for brand identity (logo, marketing surfaces). Interactive UI (buttons, links, focus rings, selected states) uses color.info, which aliases per-stop to color.brand — one blue, two semantic names. Primary button fill and selectedFill both resolve to brand.750 (#007CA0), the AA-safe stop added so white text clears 4.5:1 in both themes; .500 itself is not used directly for interactive fills. Update .500 to shift the base hue, or .750 to adjust interactive contrast.

950-50.$description: brand.950 at 50% opacity — for status.info.bg in dark mode.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Aliased to color.brand. There is one blue in the Nango system — the brand cyan — and color.info exists only as a semantic name for interactive UI (buttons, focus rings, links, selected states, info alerts). Update color.brand to change the blue everywhere. Focus shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0,178,227,0.2).

950-50.$description: brand.950 at 50% opacity — for status.info.bg in dark mode.

750.$description: AA-safe icon stop, added to fix icon.success reading too dark in light mode. .700 (#1E9E3E) is the vibrant success green but only reaches 2.93:1 on surface.panelInset (#EBEBEB) — fails the 3:1 UI-component threshold on that one surface. .800 (#1A6B2C) passes everywhere (5.53–6.60:1) but reads as a muddy dark green, losing the 'healthy/connected' visual signal — this is what caused the light-mode 'icons look very dark' report. At .750, contrast is 3.29:1 on panelInset and 3.93:1 on white/page — clears 3:1 with margin on every light surface while staying visibly closer to the vibrant .700 green. Mirrors the color.brand.750 precedent (added for the same reason on the primary button). Used as the single status.success.icon value in Light theme.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Success ramp — connected, healthy, complete states. Sourced from --color-green-* in webapp index.css. 500=#4CD964, 700=#1E9E3E, 750=#1D943A (icon-only AA-safe stop), 900=#0A3D16.

950-50.$description: success.950 at 50% opacity — for status.success.bg in dark mode.

650.$description: Closest stop to warning.500 that still clears 3:1 on every light surface — added 2026-07-09 per request for a less-brown, more-yellow icon color. Holds warning.500's hue and saturation (H≈43°, S≈91%) and darkens lightness only as far as needed: 3.12:1 on surface.panelInset (the tightest surface), 3.72:1 on white. .700 (#A07B10) is the same hue family but noticeably less saturated/lighter-shifted, which is what read as 'brown' rather than yellow. Any lighter than this (toward .600 #E6B738) drops below 3:1. Used as the single status.warning.icon value in Light theme.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Warning ramp — caution, degraded, retry, non-blocking alerts. Sourced from --color-yellow-* in webapp index.css. 500=#F7C752, 650=#AC7C08 (icon-only, closest-to-500 AA-safe stop), 700=#D6A81E, 900=#5C4A0B.

950-50.$description: warning.950 at 50% opacity — for status.warning.bg in dark mode.

975.$description: Extra-dark stop — used for alert/banner bg in dark mode where .950 is too saturated.

$description.$value: Danger ramp — errors, failures, destructive actions. Sourced from --color-red-* in webapp index.css. 500=#EE4242, 700=#E81818, 900=#5A0F0F. Focus shadow for error states: 0 0 0 3px rgba(238,66,66,0.2).

950-50.$description: danger.950 at 50% opacity — for status.danger.bg in dark mode.

$description.$value: Alpha color primitives for overlays and borders.

Decorative-only accents for charts, avatars, tag differentiation. Sourced from --color-blue/violet/mint/pink/yellow-500 in webapp index.css. Never use for interactive states or status semantics — use info/success/warning/danger for those.

500.$description: Decorative yellow for chart series and categorical data. Same hex as warning.500 by design (Nango brand yellow), but lives in the accent namespace so charts don't reference the semantic warning ramp. Use warning.* only for actual caution/degraded states.

$description.$value: Primitive typography tokens. Geist for UI text, Geist Mono for code, IDs, API keys, slugs, and data.

mono.$value: Geist Mono, ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, Liberation Mono, monospace

$description.$value: 4px-base spacing scale. Half-steps (0_5, 1_5, 2_5, 3_5) ordered inline.
Corner radius scale. radius.lg (8px) matches the nango.dev --radius-main.
Border width primitives.
Icon size and stroke primitives.
Transition duration and easing primitives. Default transition duration in the Nango app is 100ms (--default-transition-duration).
Box-shadow layering tokens. Note: Token Studio boxShadow type requires inline rgba — cannot reference color.alpha primitives directly.
Opacity scale — covers both element opacity (disabled, subdued) and alpha-layer composition.
Blur tokens for overlays and softened backgrounds.
Responsive layout primitives. Breakpoints sourced from webapp CSS (--breakpoint-xl: 1280px, --breakpoint-2xl: 1440px, etc.).
Nango semantic tokens — Dark theme. Mirrors the Light structure exactly; only values differ.
Background and container surfaces.

raised.$description: NEW: lifted active surface. In dark, this is meaningfully different from panel.

$description.$value: Text colors.

muted.$description: A11Y RULE: do not use at fontSize.2xs (11px) or fontSize.xs (12px) — 4.18:1 on surface.panel, 3.68:1 on panelInset. Use text.secondary at those sizes. See 'Accessibility note — text.muted at small sizes'.

disabled.$description: A11Y FIX (2026-07-09): was neutral.600 (2.93:1 on the disabled button fill, and that gap shrank further once the fill was lightened — reported as unreadable). Moved to neutral.500 (same stop as text.muted) — 3.16:1 against the new interactive.disabled fill (neutral.800).

onBrand.$description: Flipped from neutral.900 to white — required now that interactive.primary/selectedFill use info.750 (dark text on info.750 only reaches 3.68:1, fails AA; white reaches 4.78:1).

$description.$value: Borders and dividers.

interactive.$description: Resting border for interactive controls (checkboxes, toggles, segmented controls, chips) — same weight as border.stronger.

interactiveHover.$description: Hover border for interactive controls — strengthens to the same weight as border.input.

disabled.$description: A11Y FIX (2026-07-09): was alpha.white.6 (imperceptible against a near-black fill). Doubled to alpha.white.12 so the disabled button has an actual visible edge — on par with border.default (white.10) and still well below border.stronger (white.24).

$description.$value: Icon colors.

disabled.$description: A11Y FIX (2026-07-09): was neutral.600 (low contrast against the disabled fill, matched the old text.disabled report). Moved to neutral.500 to match the text.disabled fix — 3.16:1 against the new interactive.disabled fill (neutral.800).

info.$description: DEPRECATED — aliased to status.info.icon (was {color.info.400}). status.*.icon is the single source for semantic icon color; verified >=3:1 on tinted status bgs AND neutral surfaces in both themes. Re-bind layers to status.info.icon, then remove this alias.

success.$description: DEPRECATED — aliased to status.success.icon (was {color.success.400}). status.*.icon is the single source for semantic icon color; verified >=3:1 on tinted status bgs AND neutral surfaces in both themes. Re-bind layers to status.success.icon, then remove this alias.

warning.$description: DEPRECATED — aliased to status.warning.icon (was {color.warning.400}). status.*.icon is the single source for semantic icon color; verified >=3:1 on tinted status bgs AND neutral surfaces in both themes. Re-bind layers to status.warning.icon, then remove this alias.

danger.$description: DEPRECATED — aliased to status.danger.icon (was {color.danger.400}). status.*.icon is the single source for semantic icon color; verified >=3:1 on tinted status bgs AND neutral surfaces in both themes. Re-bind layers to status.danger.icon, then remove this alias.

$description.$value: Solid fill colors for buttons, switches, selected states.

primary.$description: Unified brand-blue fill — same value used in Light theme, so the primary button no longer flips between bright cyan (dark) and dark teal (light). White text (text.onBrand) reaches 4.78:1.

selectedFill.$description: Matches interactive.primary (info.750) so selected state and primary button read as the same brand blue. text.onBrand (white) passes at 4.78:1 — verified in both themes.

$description.$value: Global interactive state layers.

disabled.$description: A11Y FIX (2026-07-09): was neutral.900 (1.12:1 against surface.panel/page — fill was indistinguishable from the app background). Moved to neutral.800 (same stop as surface.overlay) for a visible recessed fill; combined with the border.disabled and text.disabled bumps, the disabled button now reads as a muted-but-legible control instead of disappearing into the dark background.

$description.$value: Status colors. NOTE: in dark, .strong tightens to color.X.300 (matches .icon) for better contrast on saturated backgrounds — this resolves the old alert.title divergence. A11Y FIX: info/success borders lightened one stop (.800→.700) to clear 3:1 against their bg; border pairs with bg, borderHover pairs with bgHover. CONSOLIDATION: status.*.icon is the canonical semantic icon color; icon.info/success/warning/danger are deprecated aliases.
Focus indicators.
Elevation aliases.
Chart series colors only.
Switch off-state has unique values per theme.

trackOff.$description: Switch track in off state — in dark, the recessed look uses inputMuted (vs. border.stronger in light).

$description.$value: Nango semantic tokens — Light theme. Foundational semantic layer only. Components reference these directly; no per-component pass-through tokens.
Background and container surfaces.

raised.$description: NEW: lifted active surface (active tab pill, active toggle item). Same as panel in light, distinct from panel in dark.

$description.$value: Text colors.

muted.$description: A11Y RULE: do not use at fontSize.2xs (11px) or fontSize.xs (12px) — fails 4.5:1 on light surfaces (4.28:1 on surface.page). Use text.secondary at those sizes. See 'Accessibility note — text.muted at small sizes'.

disabled.$description: A11Y FIX (2026-07-09): was neutral.400 (2.34:1 on the disabled button fill — reported as unreadable). Moved to neutral.500 (same stop as text.muted) — clears 3:1 against the new interactive.disabled fill (neutral.100). Disabled-ness is still communicated by the muted fill/border and cursor, not by making the label illegibly faint.

$description.$value: Borders and dividers.

interactive.$description: Resting border for interactive controls (checkboxes, toggles, segmented controls, chips) — same weight as border.stronger.

interactiveHover.$description: Hover border for interactive controls — strengthens to the same weight as border.input.

disabled.$description: A11Y FIX (2026-07-09): was neutral.100 (1.19:1 against surface.panel — no visible edge). Moved to neutral.300 so the disabled button silhouette is actually readable as a button outline instead of vanishing into the page.

$description.$value: Icon colors.

disabled.$description: A11Y FIX (2026-07-09): was neutral.300 (~1.6:1 on the disabled button fill). Moved to neutral.450 (~2.8:1 against the new interactive.disabled fill) — kept one stop lighter than text.disabled so icons stay slightly more muted than labels, per the existing icon/text hierarchy.

info.$description: DEPRECATED — aliased to status.info.icon (now {color.info.800}, unified with text). Re-bind layers to status.info.icon, then remove this alias.

success.$description: DEPRECATED — aliased to status.success.icon (now {color.success.750}, split from text for contrast reasons — see status.success.icon description). Re-bind layers to status.success.icon, then remove this alias.

warning.$description: DEPRECATED — aliased to status.warning.icon (now {color.warning.650}, split from text for contrast reasons — see status.warning.icon description). Re-bind layers to status.warning.icon, then remove this alias.

danger.$description: DEPRECATED — aliased to status.danger.icon (now {color.danger.700}, split from text for contrast reasons — see status.danger.icon description). Re-bind layers to status.danger.icon, then remove this alias.

$description.$value: Solid fill colors for buttons, switches, selected states. Variants compose at the component level — no button.primary/button.danger split.

primary.$description: Unified brand-blue fill — same value used in Dark theme. White text (text.onBrand) reaches 4.78:1.

selectedFill.$description: Matches interactive.primary (info.750) so selected state and primary button read as the same brand blue. text.onBrand (white) passes at 4.78:1 — verified in both themes.

$description.$value: Global interactive state layers — applied uniformly across all components.

disabled.$description: A11Y FIX (2026-07-09): was neutral.50 (1.09:1 against surface.panel — fill was indistinguishable from the page). Moved to neutral.100 for a visible recessed fill; combined with the border.disabled and text.disabled bumps, the disabled button now reads as a muted-but-legible control instead of disappearing.

$description.$value: Status colors for badges, alerts, banners, and any semantic indicator. bg = surface, border = outline, icon = accent, text = body text, strong = headings/titles. A11Y FIX: text moved .700→.800 (body text needs 4.5:1 on tinted bg — .700 hit 3.31–4.28:1); strong moved .800→.900 to preserve hierarchy. ICON FIX (2026-07-09): icon was briefly unified with text at .800, which passed contrast everywhere but read as dark/muddy on light surfaces (reported: icons look 'very dark' in light mode vs. vibrant in dark mode). Icons only need the 3:1 UI-component threshold, not text's 4.5:1, so icon has been split back out to the most vibrant stop that still clears 3:1 on every light surface (page/panel/panelInset/status.*.bg): warning.700, danger.700, and a new success.750 stop (success.700 alone falls to 2.93:1 on panelInset). CONSOLIDATION: status.*.icon is the canonical semantic icon color; icon.info/success/warning/danger are deprecated aliases pointing to it.

bg.$description: A11Y/VISIBILITY FIX (2026-07-22): was info.50 (#EEFCFE) — nearly indistinguishable from white in light mode, reported as too dim. Moved to .100. text/icon at .800 still clears AA (5.68:1).

bgHover.$description: Bumped .100→.200 (2026-07-22) alongside the bg fix so hover still reads as a visible deepen. Now matches status.info.border (#A3E6F6) — border blends into the fill on hover, which is an accepted trade-off.

icon.$description: Icon color for info status. Unified with status.info.text at .800 — both pass 4.5:1 on white and on info.bg in light mode. Dark mode uses .400 for both (already unified).

bg.$description: A11Y/VISIBILITY FIX (2026-07-22): was success.50 (#F2FBF5) — nearly indistinguishable from white in light mode, reported as too dim. Moved to .100. text at .800 still clears AA (5.93:1); icon at .750 still clears 3:1 (3.53:1).

icon.$description: Icon color for success status. Split from status.success.text (.800) on 2026-07-09 — icon only needs 3:1 (UI component), not text's 4.5:1, and .800 read as muddy dark green in light mode. .750 gives 3.29:1 on panelInset / 3.93:1 on white — passes 3:1 everywhere while staying visibly closer to the vibrant success.700 green. Dark mode is unaffected (icon stays {color.success.400}, already vibrant against dark surfaces).

bgHover.$description: Bumped .100→.200 (2026-07-22) alongside the bg fix so hover still reads as a visible deepen. Now matches status.success.border (#C7EED2) — border blends into the fill on hover, which is an accepted trade-off.

bg.$description: A11Y/VISIBILITY FIX (2026-07-22): was warning.50 (#FFFBE8) — nearly indistinguishable from white in light mode, reported as too dim. Moved to .100. text at .800 still clears AA (5.71:1); icon at .650 still clears 3:1 (3.47:1).

icon.$description: Icon color for warning status. Split from status.warning.text (.800) on 2026-07-09 — icon only needs 3:1 (UI component), not text's 4.5:1. First moved to .700 (#A07B10, 3.30–3.94:1), then to .650 (#AC7C08, 3.12–3.72:1) per follow-up request for a less-brown, more-yellow read — .650 holds warning.500's hue/saturation and only darkens as far as needed to clear 3:1 on the tightest surface (panelInset). .800 (#7A5E0A) reads as olive/brown, losing the caution signal, which is what caused the original light-mode 'too dark' report. Dark mode is unaffected (icon stays {color.warning.400}, already vibrant against dark surfaces).

bgHover.$description: Bumped .100→.200 (2026-07-22) alongside the bg fix so hover still reads as a visible deepen. Distinct from status.warning.border (#FED75B/.300) — no collision here, unlike info/success/danger.

bg.$description: A11Y/VISIBILITY FIX (2026-07-22): was danger.50 (#FEF5F5) — nearly indistinguishable from white in light mode, reported as too dim. Moved to .100. text at .700 still clears AA (4.02:1 for bold/large text; see status.danger.text); icon at .700 still clears 3:1 (4.02:1).

icon.$description: Icon color for danger status. Split from status.danger.text (.800) on 2026-07-09 — icon only needs 3:1 (UI component), not text's 4.5:1. .700 (#E81818) is the vibrant alert-red and clears 3:1 on every light surface with margin (3.85–4.59:1 — it even clears 4.5:1); .800 (#A11313) reads as a duller brick red, which is what caused the light-mode 'too dark' report. Dark mode is unaffected (icon stays {color.danger.400}, already vibrant against dark surfaces).

text.$description: UNIFIED WITH ICON (2026-07-22): was danger.800 (#A11313) — the Badge/Alert danger icon and text were rendering as two visibly different reds. Moved text to .700 (#E81818) to match status.danger.icon. Caveat: .700 on the new .100 bg = 4.02:1, under 4.5:1 for regular body text — fine for bold/large text (3:1 threshold), but check AA if this token is ever used for small regular-weight copy.

bgHover.$description: Bumped .100→.200 (2026-07-22) alongside the bg fix so hover still reads as a visible deepen. Now matches status.danger.border (#FAC9C9) — border blends into the fill on hover, which is an accepted trade-off.

$description.$value: Focus indicators.
Elevation aliases for common container roles.

Chart series colors only — these are real assignment decisions (which accent maps to which series). All other chart visual roles use foundational tokens directly (gridline=border.muted, axis.label=text.secondary, etc.).
Reserved for the few cases where form controls diverge from foundational tokens. Currently: switch off-state has unique values per theme.

trackOff.$description: Switch track in off state — divergent in dark mode (uses inputMuted instead of border).

$description.$value: Composite typography tokens. Theme-agnostic — identical in Light and Dark. Renamed from the old Body/* hierarchy. Distinction between text and label is line height (text = normal 1.5, label = snug 1.35).

lg.$description: 24px / Regular / 1.2 LH / -0.01em — page headings, hero titles
14px / Medium / 1.35 LH / -0.01em — large labels (form labels, prominent chips)

md.$description: 13px / Medium / 1.35 LH / -0.01em — standard labels (badges, table headers)

sm.$description: 12px / Regular / 1.35 LH / -0.01em — small labels (metadata, helper text)

xs.$description: 11px / Regular / 1.35 LH / -0.01em — micro labels (tag content, inline status)

xxs.$description: 10px / Regular / 1.35 LH / -0.01em — finest micro labels — use sparingly

sm.$description: 12px / Geist Mono Regular / 1.5 LH — inline code, IDs, slugs

xs.$description: 11px / Geist Mono Regular / 1.5 LH — small inline code

xxs.$description: 10px / Geist Mono Regular / 1.5 LH / -0.16em — finest mono (extra letter-spacing tightening intentional for legibility)

md.$description: 13px / Geist Mono Medium / 1.5 LH — emphasized code (was misnamed 'Body/Code/regular/md' — actual weight is Medium)

sm.$description: 12px / Geist Mono Medium / 1.5 LH — emphasized inline code

xs.$description: 11px / Geist Mono Medium / 1.5 LH — emphasized small inline code

xxs.$description: 10px / Geist Mono Medium / 1.5 LH — emphasized finest mono
